title: WU 2.10-7: Sweep + tag m1-phase02-10 (Z80 ISA complete)
description: Final verification. Clean build, opcodes count higher by ~51. Sanity-check key FUSE cases: ed 44 (NEG), ed 47 (LD I,A), ed 57 (LD A,I), ed a0 (LDI), ed b0 (LDIR), ed b1 (CPIR), d3 (OUT), db (IN). Tag m1-phase02-10. After this WU the documented Z80 ISA is complete; only Phase 2.11 (programs + flag tightening) remains for M1. Plan WU 2.10-7. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-6: I/O — IN/OUT (18 opcodes via 12 classes)
description: 12 Op classes covering 18 opcodes: InAImm/OutImmA (main 0xDB/0xD3, port=(A shl 8)|n, R+=1, 11T), InRC parameterized (ED 40+8*r, port=cpu.bc, sets flags from byte read), OutCR parameterized (ED 41+8*r), InCFlags singleton (ED 70 — flags only, no register write), OutCZero singleton (ED 71 — writes 0). Block I/O: Ini/Ind/Inir/Indr (use cpu.io.read), Outi/Outd/Otir/Otdr (use cpu.io.write). Block I/O standard documented flags only (S/H/PV undocumented bits left at 0). Extend EdOps + EdOpsTest. ~30 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-6. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-5: Block compare CPI/CPD/CPIR/CPDR (4 opcodes)
description: 4 singleton Op classes. Same shape as block move but compare A with mem[HL] instead of copying. Use Flags.afterSub(A, mem[HL], 0) for flag computation; do NOT update A (CP semantics). HL ± 1, BC -= 1. CPIR/CPDR exit when BC == 0 OR when match found (Z set). Loop iteration 21T; exit 16T. PC handling like LDIR. Flags: S/Z/H/N from afterSub; PV=(BC!=0 after); C preserved. Extend EdOps + EdOpsTest. ~14 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-5. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-4: Block move LDI/LDD/LDIR/LDDR (4 opcodes — exercises Trap B fix)
description: 4 singleton Op classes. LDI/LDD: one-shot block copy. LDIR/LDDR: looping variants — each execute() does ONE iteration; if BC != 0 leave PC unchanged (so next dispatch re-fires) and use 21T; if BC == 0 advance PC by 2 and use 16T. Trap B fix in FuseSuite drives the loop. Flags: H=0, N=0, P/V=(BC!=0 after); S/Z/C preserved. CRITICAL: Ldir test runs execute() twice manually to verify PC behavior at both points. Extend EdOps + EdOpsTest. ~16 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-4. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-2: Register transfers + NEG + RETI/RETN + RRD/RLD (8 opcodes)
description: 8 singleton Op classes: LdIA/LdRA (no flags), LdAI/LdAR (compute flags incl. P/V from iff2 — easy to forget). Neg delegates to Flags.afterSub(0,A,0). Reti/Retn pop pc; Retn also restores iff1 from iff2. Rrd/Rld rotate decimal nibbles between A and (HL); compute S/Z/PV/H=0/N=0/C preserved. Extend EdOps + EdOpsTest. ~28 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-2. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-1: IoBus + Cpu.io + Trap B fix + EdOps skeleton
description: Foundation for Phase 2.10. Create IoBus interface + NoIoBus default singleton + Cpu.io field. Apply Trap B fix to FuseSuite.runOne: replace one-shot dispatch with 'while cpu.tStates < startTStates + tStatesToRun' loop. Create empty EdOps fragment + wire into OpTableBuilder. Critical: Trap B test verifies multiple-iteration dispatch works for stub Ops without breaking single-iteration ops. ~6 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-1. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: WU 2.10-3: Extended LD pair to/from memory (8 opcodes)
description: Two parameterized classes over RegPair: LdAddrFromPair (ED 43/53/63/73, 20T) writes pair to memory; LdPairFromAddr (ED 4B/5B/6B/7B, 20T) reads from memory into pair. ED 63/6B duplicate main-table 0x22/0x2A but at higher cycle cost (20T vs 16T). No flag changes. Validate pair != AF in init. Extend EdOps + EdOpsTest. ~10 new tests. Plan WU 2.10-3. Epic: zx80-15w.

title: Phase 2.10: ED remainder + I/O + Trap B fix
description: Complete the documented Z80 instruction set. ~51 ED-prefixed and main-table-straggler opcodes across ~28 Op classes. Two harness foundations: IoBus interface + Cpu.io field, and the long-deferred Trap B fix to FuseSuite.runOne (loop until tStatesToRun reached so block ops can run multiple iterations within one FUSE case). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-10-ed-remainder-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-10-ed-remainder.md. Phase 2.9 (epic zx80-ux1) must complete first. End milestone tag: m1-phase02-10.
close_reason: Phase 2.10 complete: ED remainder + I/O + Trap B fix. ~51 opcodes, opcodes count 700/1792, SCORE 0.430, FUSE 487/1356. Z80 documented ISA implementation complete.

title: WU 2.9-4: ResIxd + SetIxd (32 documented (IX+d)/(IY+d) bit ops)
description: Two Op classes parameterized over (IndexReg, n in 0..7). ResIxd at rrr=110 slots in 0x80-0xBF clears bit n; SetIxd at 0xC0-0xFF sets bit n. 8 n × 2 IndexReg × 2 ops = 32 opcodes. 23T (read + modify + write). No flags affected. Both validate n in 0..7. Extend IxCbOps + IxCbOpsTest. After this WU, DDCB/FDCB have 31 documented opcodes each = 62 total. ~13 new tests. Plan WU 2.9-4. Epic: zx80-ux1.

title: WU 2.9-5: Sweep + tag m1-phase02-9
description: Final verification. Clean build, opcodes count higher by 62. Sanity-check key FUSE cases: ddcb 06 (RLC (IX+d)), ddcb 46 (BIT 0,(IX+d)), ddcb 86 (RES 0,(IX+d)), ddcb c6 (SET 0,(IX+d)). Tag m1-phase02-9. Plan WU 2.9-5. Epic: zx80-ux1.

title: WU 2.9-3: BitIxd (16 BIT n,(IX+d) opcodes)
description: BitIxd(idx, n) parameterized over (IndexReg, n in 0..7). 8 n × 2 IndexReg = 16 opcodes at rrr=110 slots in DDCB/FDCB 0x40-0x7F. CRITICAL: 20T (not 23T) since BIT only reads, doesn't write. Z=!(bit), H=1, N=0, C preserved, S/PV at 0 (undocumented). Memory unchanged. Validate n in 0..7 in init. Extend IxCbOps + IxCbOpsTest. ~9 new tests. Plan WU 2.9-3. Epic: zx80-ux1.

title: WU 2.9-1: IxCbOps fragment skeleton + wire into builder
description: Foundation for Phase 2.9. Create empty IxCbOps fragment + wire into OpTableBuilder. No Op classes yet. ~1 trivial test (skeleton produces empty ddcb/fdcb tables). Plan WU 2.9-1. Epic: zx80-ux1.

title: WU 2.9-2: RotShiftIxd (14 documented rotate/shift (IX+d) ops)
description: RotShiftIxd(idx, op) parameterized over (IndexReg, RotateOp). 7 RotateOp × 2 IndexReg = 14 opcodes installed at rrr=110 slots in DDCB/FDCB 0x00-0x3F (skipping SLL at 0x36). 23T, R+=2, PC+=4. Reuses RotateOp.apply from Phase 2.7. Extend IxCbOps + IxCbOpsTest with rotate/shift installer + assertions including SLL slots null + undocumented copy-to-r slots null. ~10 new tests. Plan WU 2.9-2. Epic: zx80-ux1.

title: Phase 2.9: DDCB/FDCB indexed bit/rotate
description: Implement 62 documented DDCB/FDCB-prefixed opcodes (indexed (IX+d)/(IY+d) variants of CB-prefixed rotate/shift/BIT/SET/RES). 4 Op classes parameterized over IndexReg. Documented opcodes only at rrr=110 slots; the other ~225 per table are undocumented copy-to-r variants and stay null. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-9-ddcb-fdcb-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-9-ddcb-fdcb.md. Phase 2.8 (epic zx80-y7g) must complete first. End milestone tag: m1-phase02-9.
close_reason: Phase 2.9 complete: 62 documented DDCB/FDCB indexed bit/rotate opcodes shipped across 4 Op classes; SCORE 0.408; m1-phase02-9 tagged.

title: WU 2.8-4: LD (IX+d),n + ALU A,(IX+d) + INC/DEC (IX+d) (22 opcodes)
description: Four Op classes: LdIxdImm (DD/FD 36, 19T, operandLength=2 with d at pc+2 and n at pc+3), AluAFromIxd (16 opcodes via AluOp parameterization, 19T, delegates to AluOp.apply), IncIxd/DecIxd (23T, preserve C flag via Flags.afterInc/afterDec). All read signed displacement and compute effective address with mod-64K wrap. Extend IxOps + IxOpsTest. ~15 new tests. Plan WU 2.8-4. Epic: zx80-y7g.

title: WU 2.8-6: Sweep + tag m1-phase02-8
description: Final verification. Clean build, opcodes count higher by ~79. Sanity-check key FUSE cases: dd 21 (LD IX,nn), dd 23 (INC IX), dd 86 (ADD A,(IX+d)), dd e9 (JP (IX)), fd 21 (LD IY,nn). Tag m1-phase02-8. Plan WU 2.8-6. Epic: zx80-y7g.

title: WU 2.8-5: JP (IX) and JP (IY) (2 opcodes)
description: JpIx(idx) singleton parameterized class. DD E9 / FD E9. 8T, R+=2. Sets pc to idx value (NOT memory dereference, despite the parens — test specifically pokes memory at idx to verify). No flag changes. Extend IxOps + IxOpsTest. ~6 new tests. Plan WU 2.8-5. Epic: zx80-y7g.

title: WU 2.8-3: LD r,(IX+d) and LD (IX+d),r (28 opcodes)
description: Two Op classes parameterized over (IndexReg, Reg): LdRegFromIxd and LdIxdFromReg. 7 register × 2 index × 2 directions = 28 opcodes. Signed 8-bit displacement at pc+2; effective address (idx + signedD) and 0xFFFF (wraps mod 64K). 19T, R+=2, PC+=3. No flags. Skip rrr=110 (would be (HL) which makes no sense here). Extend IxOps + IxOpsTest. ~12 new tests. Plan WU 2.8-3. Epic: zx80-y7g.

title: WU 2.8-2: Pair-touching IX/IY ops (10 classes, 26 opcodes)
description: 10 Op classes parameterized over IndexReg: LdIxImm, LdIxFromAddr, LdAddrFromIx, IncIx, DecIx, AddIxPair, PushIx, PopIx, ExSpIx, LdSpFromIx. AddIxPair has the wrinkle that srcBits=2 means 'idx itself' (not HL). All 13 opcodes per index = 26 across DD/FD. T-states match HL counterpart + 4. Each Op installs at both DD and FD via IxOps.installPairTouching. ~50 new tests. Plan WU 2.8-2. Epic: zx80-y7g.

title: WU 2.8-1: IndexReg enum + IxOps skeleton + LD SP,HL straggler
description: Foundation for Phase 2.8. Add IndexReg enum (IX/IY) with read/write/mnemonic. Create LdSpHl singleton (main 0xF9, 6T) — the straggler from Phase 2.5 that lands here alongside LD SP,IX/IY. Create IxOps fragment skeleton + wire into OpTableBuilder. Skeleton only installs LdSpHl at main 0xF9; dd/fd populated by later WUs. ~8 new tests. Plan WU 2.8-1. Epic: zx80-y7g.

title: Phase 2.8: DD/FD-prefixed IX/IY
description: Implement DD/FD-prefixed IX/IY opcode tables. ~79 documented opcodes across ~18 Op classes. Most are mechanical adaptations of HL-based ops with +4 T-state penalty for the prefix and signed 8-bit displacement for (IX+d)/(IY+d) addressing. Foundation: IndexReg enum (IX/IY). Includes straggler LD SP,HL (0xF9 main). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-8-ix-iy-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-8-ix-iy.md. Phase 2.7 (epic zx80-xz5) must complete first. End milestone tag: m1-phase02-8.
close_reason: Phase 2.8 complete: 6 WUs closed, ~79 DD/FD-prefixed IX/IY opcodes implemented, m1-phase02-8 tag applied, SCORE 0.381 (587/1792 opcodes)

title: WU 2.7-6: Sweep + tag m1-phase02-7
description: Final verification. Clean build, opcodes count higher by 248. Sanity-check FUSE cb-00, cb-40, cb-80, cb-c0 all pass (one per CB block). Tag m1-phase02-7. Plan WU 2.7-6. Epic: zx80-xz5.
close_reason: Phase 2.7 swept: 248 CB opcodes added (260->508), tests 534, SCORE 0.342, tag m1-phase02-7 placed. FUSE cb 00/40/80/c0 all PASS.

title: WU 2.7-4: RES n,r and RES n,(HL) — 64 CB opcodes
description: ResReg(n,dst) is 8T; ResHl(n) is 15T. Clear bit n; no flags affected. Both validate n in 0..7. Extend CbOps + CbOpsTest. ~12 new tests. Plan WU 2.7-4. Epic: zx80-xz5.

title: WU 2.7-5: SET n,r and SET n,(HL) — 64 CB opcodes
description: SetReg(n,dst) is 8T; SetHl(n) is 15T. Set bit n; no flags affected. Both validate n in 0..7. Extend CbOps + CbOpsTest. After this WU, CB table has 248 documented opcodes installed (8 SLL slots null). ~12 new tests. Plan WU 2.7-5. Epic: zx80-xz5.

title: WU 2.7-3: BIT n,r and BIT n,(HL) — 64 CB opcodes
description: BitReg(n,src) is 8T; BitHl(n) is 12T (BIT only reads, doesn't write — distinct from SET/RES (HL) which are 15T). Z set if tested bit is 0; H always 1; N=0; C preserved; S/PV left at 0 (undocumented). Register/memory NOT modified. Both validate n in 0..7. Extend CbOps + CbOpsTest. ~13 new tests. Plan WU 2.7-3. Epic: zx80-xz5.

title: WU 2.7-2: RotShiftReg + RotShiftHl + CbOps skeleton (56 opcodes)
description: Two Op classes: RotShiftReg(op,src) covers 49 register-target opcodes (8T); RotShiftHl(op) covers 7 (HL) variants (15T). Both PC+=2, R+=2 (CB-prefixed). Create CbOps fragment with installRotateShift loop that SKIPS oooBits==6 (SLL). Wire into OpTableBuilder. ~12 new tests including assertion that SLL slots (CB 0x30-0x37) remain null. Plan WU 2.7-2. Epic: zx80-xz5.

title: WU 2.7-1: RotateOp enum + 7 Flags helpers
description: Foundation for Phase 2.7. Add 7 Flags helpers (afterRlc, afterRrc, afterRl, afterRr, afterSla, afterSra, afterSrl) sharing private computeRotateShiftFlags. Each computes S/Z/PV/H=0/N=0/C from result and the bit shifted out. Add RotateOp enum (7 values + apply + fromBits that REJECTS bits=6 SLL). ~28 new tests. No Op classes. Plan WU 2.7-1. Epic: zx80-xz5.

title: Phase 2.7: CB-prefixed (rotates/shifts/BIT/SET/RES)
description: Implement the entire CB-prefixed table — 248 documented opcodes across 8 Op classes. The 8 SLL slots (CB 0x30-0x37) stay null per the project's documented-Z80-only non-goal. Biggest single batch in M1. Foundation: RotateOp enum + 7 Flags helpers. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-7-cb-prefixed-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-7-cb-prefixed.md. Phase 2.6 (epic zx80-469) must complete first. End milestone tag: m1-phase02-7.
close_reason: Phase 2.7 complete: 248 CB opcodes (rotate/shift/BIT/RES/SET) across 8 Op classes + RotateOp enum + 7 Flags helpers. Tests 534, opcodes 508/1792 (+248), SCORE 0.342 (was 0.239). FUSE pass count 359/1356. Tag m1-phase02-7 applied.

title: WU 2.6-4: Sweep + tag m1-phase02-6

close_reason: Sweep complete: 461 tests passing, opcodes 260/1792 (+8), score 0.239. Phase 2.6 tag applied. 48/50 remaining FUSE failures are pre-existing X/Y undoc bit issues affecting all phases; 2 are pre-existing (DJNZ, HALT). All 8 new opcodes functionally correct.

title: WU 2.6-3: Daa + Cpl + Scf + Ccf (4 misc opcodes)
description: Four singleton Op classes in op/misc/. Opcodes 0x27 (DAA), 0x2F (CPL), 0x37 (SCF), 0x3F (CCF). All 4T. CCF sets H to OLD C (counterintuitive — test it). DAA delegates to Flags.afterDaa (per-edge correctness covered by FlagsTest). Extend MiscOps + MiscOpsTest. ~12 tests. Plan WU 2.6-3. Epic: zx80-469.

title: WU 2.6-2: Rlca + Rrca + Rla + Rra (4 rotate-A opcodes)
description: Four singleton Op classes in op/rot/. Opcodes 0x07 (RLCA), 0x0F (RRCA), 0x17 (RLA), 0x1F (RRA). All 4T, preserve S/Z/PV. RLA/RRA fold OLD C bit IN as new low/high bit (test with C=1). Create new RotOps fragment + wire into OpTableBuilder. ~17 tests. Plan WU 2.6-2. Epic: zx80-469.

title: WU 2.6-1: Flags helpers (afterRotateA, afterDaa, afterCpl, afterScf, afterCcf)
description: Foundation for Phase 2.6. Add 5 Flags helpers. afterRotateA preserves S/Z/PV, sets C from newC, clears H/N. afterDaa is the BCD-adjust algorithm (~30 LOC, 8+ boundary tests covering add/sub paths, half-carry, full-byte overflow). afterCpl preserves S/Z/PV/C, sets H/N. afterScf and afterCcf return Int (just new F; A unchanged). ~30 new tests with most density on DAA. Plan WU 2.6-1. Epic: zx80-469.

title: Phase 2.6: rotates on A + DAA/CPL/SCF/CCF
description: Implement 8 main-table single-byte opcodes operating on A or flags: RLCA, RRCA, RLA, RRA, DAA, CPL, SCF, CCF. Foundation: 5 Flags helpers including afterDaa (standard table-based BCD-adjust algorithm with dense per-boundary testing). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-6-rotates-a-and-misc-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-6-rotates-a-and-misc.md. Phase 2.5 (epic zx80-2uy) must complete first. End milestone tag: m1-phase02-6.
close_reason: Phase 2.6 complete: 8 opcodes (RLCA/RRCA/RLA/RRA/DAA/CPL/SCF/CCF), 5 new Flags helpers, ~50 new tests, total 461 passing. Tag m1-phase02-6 applied. Score 0.239 (opcodes 260/1792). DAA algorithm corrected to apply high-nibble correction in N=1 path (matches real Z80 hardware vs textbook).

title: WU 2.5-1: RegPair extension (AF + fromPushPopBits) + StackOps skeleton
description: Foundation for Phase 2.5. Add 5th RegPair value AF (read/writes cpu.af). Add fromPushPopBits(bits) companion (00=BC, 01=DE, 10=HL, 11=AF). Existing fromBits unchanged (still maps 11=SP). Create empty StackOps fragment + wire into OpTableBuilder. ~6 new tests. No Op classes. Plan WU 2.5-1. Epic: zx80-2uy.

title: WU 2.5-2: PushPair + PopPair (8 opcodes)
description: Two Op classes: PushPair(src) at 0xC5/D5/E5/F5 (11T); PopPair(dst) at 0xC1/D1/E1/F1 (10T). Both parameterized over RegPair, both reject SP via init guard. Use cpu.push/cpu.pop from Phase 2.4. POP AF loads F directly from popped low byte (test verifies). Fill in StackOps installer + StackOpsTest. ~14 new tests. Plan WU 2.5-2. Epic: zx80-2uy.

title: WU 2.5-3: Sweep + tag m1-phase02-5
description: Final verification. Clean build, opcodes count higher by 8. Sanity-check FUSE C5 case (PUSH BC) is not in failures. Tag m1-phase02-5. Plan WU 2.5-3. Epic: zx80-2uy.

title: Phase 2.5: PUSH/POP rr
description: Implement Z80 16-bit stack push/pop for register pairs (PUSH/POP BC/DE/HL/AF). 8 opcodes across 2 Op classes. Reuses Cpu.push/pop helpers from Phase 2.4. Foundation: extend RegPair enum with AF value + fromPushPopBits helper (PUSH/POP encoding differs from LD rr,nn). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-5-push-pop-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-5-push-pop.md. Phase 2.4 (epic zx80-b5i) must complete first. End milestone tag: m1-phase02-5.
close_reason: Phase 2.5 complete: PUSH/POP rr (BC/DE/HL/AF), 8 opcodes, SCORE 0.237, tag m1-phase02-5

title: WU 2.4-5: RET + RST family (17 opcodes)
description: Three Op classes: Ret (0xC9, 10T, pops pc), RetCc(cond) (8 opcodes 0xC0/C8/D0/D8/E0/E8/F0/F8, 5 not-taken/11 taken), Rst(target) (8 opcodes 0xC7/CF/D7/DF/E7/EF/F7/FF; pushes pc+1; target must be one of 0x00/08/10/18/20/28/30/38; 11T). Mnemonic for RST is e.g. 'RST 18H'. Extend BranchOps + BranchOpsTest. ~22 new tests. Plan WU 2.4-5. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: WU 2.4-4: CALL family (9 opcodes)
description: Two Op classes: CallAbs (0xCD, 17T, pushes pc+3 then jumps), CallAbsCc(cond) (8 opcodes 0xC4/CC/D4/DC/E4/EC/F4/FC, 10 not-taken/17 taken; push happens only on taken branch). Always reads nn even when not taken. Extend BranchOps + BranchOpsTest. ~13 new tests. Plan WU 2.4-4. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: WU 2.4-6: Sweep + tag m1-phase02-4
description: Final verification. Clean build, capture exact zx80 score headline (opcodes count higher by 42). Sanity-check FUSE C3 case (JP nn) is not in failures. Tag m1-phase02-4. Plan WU 2.4-6. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: WU 2.4-1: Condition enum + Cpu.push/pop + BranchOps skeleton
description: Foundation for Phase 2.4. Create Condition enum (8 codes: NZ/Z/NC/C/PO/PE/P/M) with test(cpu), fromBits(0..7). Create Cpu.push(mem,value) and Cpu.pop(mem):Int extension methods (pre-decrement push, post-increment pop, little-endian, SP wraps). Create empty BranchOps fragment + wire into OpTableBuilder. ~18 new tests. No Op classes. Plan WU 2.4-1. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: WU 2.4-3: JR + DJNZ family (6 opcodes, signed displacement)
description: Three Op classes: JrRel (0x18, 12T), JrRelCc(cond) (4 opcodes 0x20/28/30/38; only NZ/Z/NC/C valid; 7 not-taken/12 taken), Djnz (0x10; decrements B WITHOUT updating flags; 8 not-taken/13 taken). Displacement is signed 8-bit (mem.read().toByte().toInt()). Extend BranchOps + BranchOpsTest. ~20 new tests. Plan WU 2.4-3. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: WU 2.4-2: JP family (10 opcodes)
description: Three Op classes: JpAbs (0xC3, 10T), JpAbsCc(cond) (8 opcodes 0xC2/CA/D2/DA/E2/EA/F2/FA, 10T constant taken/not-taken), JpHl (0xE9, 4T). JpHl sets pc=cpu.hl (NOT memory[HL]). Extend BranchOps + BranchOpsTest. ~17 new tests. Plan WU 2.4-2. Epic: zx80-b5i.

title: Phase 2.4: jumps, calls, returns
description: Implement Z80 control-flow opcodes: JP, JR, DJNZ, CALL, RET, RST and conditional variants. 42 new opcode positions across 11 Op classes. None affect flags. Foundation: Condition enum + Cpu.push/pop helpers. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-4-jumps-calls-design.md. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-zx80-m1-phase2-4-jumps-calls.md. Phase 2.3 (epic zx80-vt6) must complete first. End milestone tag: m1-phase02-4.

title: WU 2.3-4: AdcHlPair + SbcHlPair (8 ED-prefixed opcodes)
description: Two Op classes parameterized over RegPair, ED-prefixed. 8 opcodes (ADC HL,rr at ED 0x4A/0x5A/0x6A/0x7A; SBC HL,rr at ED 0x42/0x52/0x62/0x72). PC+=2, R+=2 (cpu.bumpR(2)), 15 T-states. Full flag update. Extend AluOps + AluOpsTest. ~12 tests. Plan WU 2.3-4. Epic: zx80-vt6.

title: WU 2.3-3: IncPair + DecPair (8 opcodes, NO flag changes)
description: Two Op classes parameterized over RegPair. 4+4=8 opcodes (INC rr at 0x03/0x13/0x23/0x33; DEC rr at 0x0B/0x1B/0x2B/0x3B). 6 T-states. CRITICAL: 16-bit INC/DEC do NOT affect any flag (unlike 8-bit INC/DEC which preserve only C). Tests set f=0xFF before and assert f=0xFF after. Extend AluOps + AluOpsTest. ~12 tests. Plan WU 2.3-3. title: M2.4: Display backend — host window for ULA framebuffer
description: Choose UI toolkit (JavaFX / Compose Multiplatform+Skia / Swing-AWT / lwjgl) and render the ULA BufferedImage to a host window. Big architecture call. JavaFX is mature but legacy; Compose+Skia is modern but more setup; Swing is simplest but dated. Brainstorm + spec + plan when picked up. Open questions: full-screen vs windowed, scaling (1x/2x/3x/integer-scale), aspect ratio, vsync.

title: M2.5: Keyboard matrix + IoBus extension for IN ()
description: Spectrum keyboard is an 8x5 matrix read via IN port whose high byte selects the row. Need to map host key events (from M2.4's UI toolkit) to the matrix and respond to IN reads. Existing IoBus from M1 is the integration point. Brainstorm + spec + plan when picked up. Open questions: key mapping (PC keyboard -> Spectrum matrix), shift/symbol shift overlay strategy, key repeat behavior.

title: M2.2: Frame timer + 50Hz interrupt + cycle budget per frame
description: Spectrum runs at 3.5MHz / 50Hz = 69,888 T-states per frame. Each frame: run CPU until T-state budget exhausted, fire maskable INT to 0x0038, repeat. M1 already has IM/IFF/INT plumbing; need a frame timer + INT-firing harness. Brainstorm + spec + plan when picked up. Open questions: how to drive the loop (worker thread? scheduler? main thread?), how to integrate with display refresh, time-vs-cycle accuracy.

title: M2.3: ULA video — render screen RAM to BufferedImage (headless)
description: Read screen RAM at 0x4000-0x57FF (pixels) + 0x5800-0x5AFF (attributes). Generate 256x192 framebuffer + 48-pixel border. Pixel address layout is non-linear (Y bits scrambled). Attribute cells 8x8 with FLASH/BRIGHT/INK/PAPER/BORDER. FLASH cycle is 16 frames. Output to a Java BufferedImage for testability and PNG dumps. Brainstorm + spec + plan when picked up. Open questions: scanline-by-scanline vs frame-blast rendering, color palette (Spectrum has 16 colors).

title: M2.1: Memory map + 48K ROM load + write-guard at 0x4000
description: Bundle the 48K Spectrum ROM image (Sinclair original or free clone like OpenSE Basic — license decision needed). Load at 0x0000-0x3FFF on machine reset. Memory write-guard so writes to 0x0000-0x3FFF are no-ops (current Memory class allows them). Spec section: master spec line 66 ('ROM-vs-RAM distinction is a flag in M1 (not enforced) and a 0x4000 write-guard in M2'). Brainstorm + spec + plan when picked up. Open questions: which ROM, license stance, how to bundle (resource? download script?).

title: Phase 2.13 epic: DDCB/FDCB undocumented shf/rot copy-back + (set,res) on (ix,iy)+d
description: ZEXDOC progresses through all Phase 2.12 test groups OK without errors but crashes at 'shf/rot (<ix,iy>+1)' with 'no dispatch route for opcode 0xdd at pc=0x1d42'. This is the DDCB/FDCB-prefixed undocumented copy-back forms: rotate/shift on (IX+d) or (IY+d) where the result also writes back to a regular register, plus SET/RES n,(IX+d) similar copy-backs. Required to progress past 'shf/rot (<ix,iy>+1)' and reach gate 5 'shf/rot <b,c,d,e,h,l,(hl),a>' and beyond. Phase 2.12 ZEXDOC progress: 59 OK groups, 0 ERRORS, dispatch crash at shf/rot (<ix,iy>+1).
close_reason: All 4 WUs (2.13-1 through 2.13-4) closed; DDCB/FDCB tables fully populated for rotshift+RES+SET (200/256, BIT mirror out of scope); ZEXDOC clean (0 errors); m1-cpu-complete tagged.

NOT required for booting BASIC. Optional pre-M2-close work — could defer to M2.5 or M3. Brainstorm + spec + plan when picked up.

title: M2.8: Border color via OUT () low 3 bits
description: Spectrum border color comes from bits 0-2 of OUT (). Renders as the 48-pixel margin around the 256x192 main display. Coordination with M2.3 (ULA video) and M2.5 (keyboard, since same port). Brainstorm + spec + plan when picked up.

title: 2 FUSE residuals after Phase G: ed5f (LD A,R N/PV) + ed7d (RETI iff1)
description:
Two tiny FUSE failures remain after Phase G (1354/1356 = 99.85%). Both are 1-line fixes:

(1) ed5f LD A,R: 'af mismatch: 0xF3A1 vs 0xF5A1'. F bits 1 (N) and 2 (PV) differ from expected. Per Phase G subagent: appears to be an interrupt-timing / IFF2 edge case. LD A,R does N=0, PV=IFF2; expected has N=0, PV=1 vs ours N=1, PV=0. Investigate inline flag computation in LdAR.kt — the N flag arithmetic may be off, or Phase F's R-bump-ordering fix may have a small interaction. Quick triage required.

(2) ed7d RETI alias: 'iff1 mismatch'. Per undocumented Z80 docs, RETI on real hardware restores IFF1 from IFF2 identically to RETN. The pre-existing canonical Reti.kt likely doesn't restore IFF1=IFF2; Phase F oversight that only surfaced at the alias slot during Phase G's alias install. Two-line fix in Reti.execute (mirror Retn's iff1 handling).

Score impact: +2 FUSE = 0.966 -> 0.967. To pick up, brainstorm-spec-plan-execute as a small phase or just one-shot fix.

title: Phase H: fill DD/FD-as-noop opcode coverage (~296 slots)
description: Score-ceiling work. After Phase F, ~296 DD/FD-prefix slots are still null and dispatched as DdFdNopPrefix at runtime. To fill the OpcodeCoverage suite to ~100%, install proper Op classes for every DD/FD-prefix-on-main-opcode position where the unprefixed op doesn't reference H/L or (HL). Z80 hardware: prefix is a no-op with +4 T-states, +1 R increment, +1 PC. Mostly mechanical: a generic 'PrefixPassthrough' wrapper or a per-pattern install loop. Estimated ~+0.03 composite score (current 0.956 -> ~0.99). When ready, run brainstorm -> spec -> plan -> WUs flow. Filed as a single issue rather than an epic; once planned, decompose into WUs under a new epic.
